steps.title: Identify the key observation
steps.content: Reyes-Mendoza and colleagues found that water layers deeper than 400 meters in the TJBH show “density and salinity conditions akin to those of the nearby Caribbean Sea.” The word therefore signals that their suggestion must explain why a freshwater/saltwater bay hole would share those characteristics with the open Caribbean.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘there may be tunnels and caves that connect the TJBH and the waters of the Caribbean deep underground’: an underground connection directly explains how Caribbean-like conditions reached those depths. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘there are greater differences between water layers less than 275 meters deep than there are between water layers greater than 400 meters deep’: compares variation rates between depth zones, a claim not supported by or logically following from the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘researchers should reevaluate existing measurements of the depths of Watling’s Blue Hole and other blue holes where the conditions in very deep waters are similar to those of waters in open seas’: proposes re-examining other holes; nothing in the findings justifies this leap. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘the apparent relationship between depth and salinity level in the TJBH is the inverse of that found in the Caribbean Sea’: contradicts the passage, which says conditions are akin to the Caribbean, not inverse. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
